understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to progress through all levels of the KA59 puzzle game by getting pushable objects (blocks) onto goal tiles marked with '+' on the semantic grid. Each level had specific success conditions that would trigger a WIN state, allowing progression to the next level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The game used a grid-based system with pixel coordinates where x increases rightward and y increases downward, with 3 pixels per cell. Players could select different pieces using CLICK at their positions, then move them in cardinal directions using MOVE actions. Pushable blocks would shift along with the selected piece if immediately adjacent in the movement direction. Walls and boundaries blocked movement in various ways and had to be discovered through interaction. The semantic_grid provided a compact ASCII view showing the selected piece (P), other selectables (S), blocks (B), walls (#), goals (+), and empty cells (.). Each level had a finite step budget, requiring efficient use of actions.

understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to progress through all levels of the KA59 puzzle game by getting pushable objects (blocks) onto goal tiles marked with '+' on the semantic grid. Each level had specific success conditions that would trigger a WIN state, allowing progression to the next level.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: The game used a grid-based system with pixel coordinates where x increases rightward and y increases downward, with 3 pixels per cell. Players could select different pieces using CLICK at their positions, then move them in cardinal directions using MOVE actions. Pushable blocks would shift along with the selected piece if immediately adjacent in the movement direction. Walls and boundaries blocked movement in various ways and had to be discovered through interaction. The semantic_grid provided a compact ASCII view showing the selected piece (P), other selectables (S), blocks (B), walls (#), goals (+), and empty cells (.). Each level had a finite step budget, requiring efficient use of actions.
